_provenance: Claude-authored original Gaokao seven-select. Not copied from any real exam. DeepSeek not used.

sets.passage: Most of us reach automatically for our phones the very moment we wake, often before our eyes have fully opened and long before our minds are truly ready to face the busy day that lies ahead. (1)______ The bright screen floods our still sleepy eyes with urgent messages, alarming news headlines, and other people's carefully chosen highlights, all of them crowding in noisily before we have managed a single calm or original thought of our own. Researchers who study daily habits have grown increasingly worried about this restless pattern in recent years. (2)______ When the brain is forced to react to dozens of small competing demands within the very first few minutes of the morning, it quietly loses the calm space it badly needs to set clear goals and choose gentle, sensible priorities. A simple and surprisingly powerful remedy is to delay reaching for the device by just half an hour each day. (3)______ During that short protected window, you might stretch slowly, drink a tall glass of water, step outside for a breath of air, or simply watch the early light spread quietly across the wall. These plain, unhurried actions may seem almost too small to matter at all, and yet they steady the mind in a way that endless scrolling never can. Some people worry, quite naturally, that they will miss something genuinely urgent if they dare to wait. (4)______ In truth, the overwhelming majority of notifications can sit patiently for thirty short minutes without causing the smallest real harm to anyone at all. The goal here is not to reject technology entirely or to pretend that phones are somehow evil or shameful. (5)______ Instead, it is simply to decide for yourself when the noisy outside world is allowed to come rushing in, rather than letting a glowing rectangle quietly seize the most precious and unguarded moments of your entire day.
bank: Buying a brand-new and more expensive phone is clearly the most sensible answer to this problem.
Such a habit, repeated every single morning, quietly shapes the whole emotional tone of the hours that follow.
They argue that this rushed start floods us with stress hormones before the day has properly begun.
This fear, however understandable, almost always turns out to be far larger than the reality behind it.
Those tools are genuinely useful, and few of us would willingly give them up for very long.
That brief delay hands the first part of the day back to you instead of to a stream of strangers.
Sleeping for a full ten hours every night would naturally solve all of these difficulties at once.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（这种习惯每天重复，悄悄塑造之后几小时的情绪基调，承接上句早起就看手机）；空2选C（研究者担心：这种匆忙开始让压力激素先涌出，解释为何担忧）；空3选F（那短暂的延迟把一天最初的时间还给你而非陌生人，承接延迟半小时的建议）；空4选D（这种恐惧几乎总是被夸大，承接有人担心错过急事）；空5选E（这些工具确实有用，没人愿放弃，呼应不是要拒绝科技）。干扰句A、G（买新手机、睡十小时）语义相关但放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: For many long centuries, the slow and patient craft of making paper by hand has been quietly passed down from one generation to the next in a small handful of remote mountain villages. (1)______ Today, however, this delicate and ancient skill stands in real danger of vanishing entirely, as cheap factory paper floods every shop in the country and fewer and fewer young people choose to learn the old, demanding ways. The traditional process is astonishingly slow and almost stubbornly resistant to any clever modern shortcut. (2)______ Bark must be soaked for many days, beaten by hand until it turns soft, and then lifted carefully from the water on a fine bamboo screen with a single smooth, practiced motion that takes years of effort to truly master. Each finished sheet carries faint, irregular marks that no humming factory machine could ever hope to copy. (3)______ Collectors and artists prize exactly these tiny imperfections, because they prove beyond any doubt that a real human hand, and not a cold steel roller, shaped and pressed the page. Some thoughtful villages have recently begun inviting curious visitors to come and try the difficult craft for themselves. (4)______ Standing waist-deep in cold mountain water and lifting a wobbling, dripping screen for the very first time gives most outsiders a sudden, humbling respect for skills they had always carelessly taken for granted. Saving such fragile traditions, of course, asks for far more than simple nostalgia or warm, comfortable feelings alone. (5)______ Real survival depends on patient teachers, fair and honest prices, and a steady stream of thoughtful buyers who genuinely understand why a single handmade sheet may be worth a hundred ordinary ones. Only then can the gentle rhythm of the bamboo screen continue to sound for the generations still to come.
bank: These hands-on workshops have unexpectedly become one of the most effective ways to keep interest alive.
It was once a thriving trade that clothed whole communities and shaped the daily life of the region.
Importing modern paper machines from distant cities would obviously preserve the tradition far better than anything else.
Every stage, from gathering the inner bark to drying the final sheet, demands genuine patience and care.
Far from being flaws, these gentle marks are precisely what give each sheet its quiet, living character.
Mountain villages are usually colder in winter than the warm valleys that lie far below them.
Good intentions alone, sadly, have never once been enough to keep any dying craft truly breathing.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（它曾是养活整个社区的繁荣行业，承接世代相传，引出今日衰落的对比）；空2选D（每个环节都需真正的耐心，总起下文泡、捶、抄的细节）；空3选E（这些痕迹绝非瑕疵，恰是每张纸的活气，承接机器无法复制的痕迹）；空4选A（这些动手工作坊出人意料地成为保持兴趣的有效方式，承接邀请游客）；空5选G（仅有善意从不足以让濒危手艺存续，承接需要的不止怀旧）。干扰句C、F（进口造纸机、山村冬天更冷）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: When a sudden and violent flood swept through the low streets of a small riverside town early last spring, the official rescue teams, however hard they tried, could not possibly reach every threatened family in time. (1)______ Within just a few hours, ordinary residents had organized themselves into a remarkable web of volunteers, paddling small wooden boats, carrying heavy sandbags, and opening their dry upstairs rooms to soaked, exhausted, and frightened strangers. What surprised most outside observers was not the sheer scale of the damage but the astonishing speed of the human response. (2)______ Neighbors who had barely exchanged a polite nod in years suddenly found themselves working shoulder to shoulder, passing heavy buckets along a long human chain that stretched right down the flooded lane. Coordinating so many eager and willing helpers, however, turned out to be far trickier than anyone present had first expected. (3)______ Without some calm central voice to direct the milling crowd, willing volunteers sometimes rushed to the very same safe house while other, far more desperate streets were briefly and dangerously forgotten. A few quick-thinking local teenagers soon solved this exact problem in a clever and surprisingly modern way. (4)______ Using a simple shared map on their phones, they carefully marked which homes still needed urgent help and which had already been reached, turning scattered chaos into something almost orderly. When the brown water finally drew back, the grateful town discovered that it had gained something quite unexpected and genuinely lasting. (5)______ The shared hardship had quietly dissolved old suspicions and stiff distances, leaving behind a community that now genuinely knew, trusted, and looked out for itself. Disasters, it seems, can sometimes reveal the very best that ordinary people are quietly capable of becoming.
bank: So the residents simply waited indoors for the professionals to arrive and handle absolutely everything themselves.
Faced with this gap, the townspeople refused to stand by and wait helplessly for distant help.
Enthusiasm, however generous, can easily descend into confusion when nobody is clearly in charge of anything.
Floodwater is generally muddier and colder than the clean water that flows in an ordinary garden tap.
They quickly set up a basic online system to track exactly where each rescue effort was most urgently needed.
Strangers became allies almost overnight, drawn together by a single shared and frightening emergency.
Beyond the rebuilt homes, the deepest repair had taken place in the relationships between the people themselves.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（面对救援缺口，居民拒绝坐等远方援助，承接官方救援无法及时到达）；空2选F（陌生人一夜成盟友，被共同的危急联系起来，解释响应之快令人惊讶）；空3选C（再多热情，无人指挥也易陷入混乱，承接协调困难）；空4选E（他们建起简单的在线系统追踪何处最需救援，承接少年用巧妙现代办法）；空5选G（除了重建房屋，最深的修复发生在人与人的关系中，承接获得意外而持久的东西）。干扰句A、D（居民坐等专业人员、洪水比自来水浑浊）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Bees may be small and easily overlooked, but the quiet work they perform each morning underpins a surprisingly large share of the food that we eat at almost every single meal. (1)______ As a single bee moves busily from blossom to blossom in search of sweet nectar, it brushes fine pollen from one flower onto the next, quietly allowing fruits, nuts, and countless vegetables to form and ripen at all. In recent decades, however, scientists across many different countries have watched bee populations shrink at a worrying and only partly explained pace. (2)______ The likely causes are tangled together and stubbornly hard to separate from one another, ranging from heavy pesticide use to the steady loss of the wildflowers that bees depend upon for their daily food. Some people assume, quite reasonably at first, that this is a problem only for distant farmers in faraway country places. (3)______ Yet a world with far fewer bees would soon mean emptier shelves and noticeably higher prices in every ordinary supermarket, touching the dinner table of nearly every single family. Fortunately, ordinary citizens are far from powerless in the face of this slow and quiet decline. (4)______ Planting a small patch of native flowers, avoiding harsh garden chemicals, or simply leaving a shallow dish of clean water out in summer can offer real, measurable help to passing bees. Schools and city councils alike have begun joining these modest efforts with steadily growing enthusiasm. (5)______ Rooftops once covered in bare, baking gravel are now blooming brightly with lavender and clover, transforming dead urban space into welcome island refuges for tired, hungry pollinators. The lesson is gentle but perfectly clear: protecting even the smallest of creatures often turns out to protect ourselves as well.
bank: Roughly one in every three mouthfuls of our food depends, directly or indirectly, on their faithful pollination.
Honey, while certainly delicious, is actually the least important gift that these busy insects give us.
This decline is not some narrow local trouble but a quiet warning that spreads across whole continents.
Such a comforting belief, unfortunately, badly underestimates just how closely our own plates are tied to theirs.
Even the smallest balcony or windowsill can be turned into a tiny but genuine sanctuary for these insects.
Killing every bee in a region would clearly make local gardens far easier and cheaper to maintain.
Across many towns, formerly empty rooftops are being deliberately replanted as green havens for struggling bees.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选A（我们约三分之一的食物依赖其授粉，承接蜜蜂支撑大量食物）；空2选C（这种衰退不是局部小麻烦而是横跨大陆的警告，承接多国观察到数量下降）；空3选D（这种安慰性的想法低估了餐桌与蜜蜂的紧密关系，反驳只是远方农民的事）；空4选E（哪怕最小的阳台也能变成蜜蜂的庇护所，总起下文种花、留水的具体做法）；空5选G（许多城镇把空屋顶重新种成绿色港湾，承接学校与市政加入）。干扰句B、F（蜂蜜最不重要、杀光蜜蜂更省钱）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Many students believe, almost as an unquestioned article of faith, that the longer they sit and stare at their open books, the more they are surely bound to learn and remember. (1)______ Whole hours quietly melt away while the very same paragraph is read over and over again, the tired eyes sliding smoothly across familiar words while the restless mind drifts off somewhere far away. Cognitive researchers, for their part, have spent several decades carefully studying what actually makes new information stick inside the human brain. (2)______ Their findings point, again and again and again, to a simple but slightly uncomfortable truth: passive rereading is one of the very weakest study methods that anyone could possibly choose. Far more powerful, it turns out, is a technique that feels distinctly harder and is therefore often quietly avoided. (3)______ By closing the heavy book and forcing yourself to recall the key ideas entirely from memory, you make your brain genuinely work, and that very effort is exactly what carves the knowledge deeper and more permanently. Spacing your study sessions out across several separate days, rather than cramming them all into one frantic and sleepless night, brings a second and equally large advantage. (4)______ Each time you return to slightly faded material and gently pull it back into sharp focus, the memory grows noticeably stronger and lasts considerably longer than before. None of this, it must honestly be admitted, ever feels quite as comfortable as gently rereading neatly highlighted notes. (5)______ Yet genuine learning, much like genuine physical fitness, tends to grow precisely from the moments of real strain rather than from the easy, pleasant, comfortable ones. The students who quietly embrace this small discomfort are very often the very ones who, in the end, remember the most.
bank: In reality, however, sheer time spent is a remarkably poor measure of how much is genuinely understood.
Reading a chapter aloud in a loud, dramatic voice is widely proven to be the single best technique.
Self-testing, the experts agree, beats almost every other approach by a clear and consistent margin.
A short fifteen-minute nap before any exam is the surest possible route to a perfect final score.
This approach, often called retrieval practice, sounds modest but produces strikingly durable results.
Effortful study feels less rewarding in the moment but pays back generously over the long run.
Allowing a little forgetting to happen between sessions actually strengthens the final memory rather than weakening it.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选A（其实花的时间是衡量理解程度的差指标，反驳看得越久学得越多）；空2选C（专家一致认为自我测验胜过几乎所有方法，承接研究什么真正记得住）；空3选E（这种被称为提取练习的方法朴素却效果持久，承接合上书凭记忆回想）；空4选G（让两次复习间发生一点遗忘反而强化最终记忆，承接分散学习的第二个好处）；空5选F（费力的学习当下回报少但长期收益大，呼应不如重读舒服）。干扰句B、D（大声朗读最佳、考前小睡保满分）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: High in the thin, freezing air of the world's tallest and most forbidding mountains lives a shy grey cat that almost no ordinary human has ever once seen in the wild. (1)______ The snow leopard moves like a soft and silent ghost across the steep rock and glittering ice, its thick smoky coat blending so perfectly with the bare stone that even patient, well-trained scientists can stare straight at one and somehow miss it. For many long years, this extraordinary animal remained one of the very least understood large predators anywhere on the entire planet. (2)______ Its remote and lonely home, often far above the height where any ordinary trees can grow, kept curious researchers firmly away and let the cat guard its many secrets in near-total silence. Modern technology, however, has slowly begun to lift this ancient and stubborn veil of mystery. (3)______ Hidden cameras, triggered by the very faintest movement, now quietly capture the leopard padding softly through moonlit mountain passes, while tiny satellite collars trace journeys far longer than anyone had ever once dared to imagine. What these remarkable new tools reveal is an animal living under genuine and steadily growing pressure. (4)______ As herders push their hungry flocks ever higher up the slopes and the warming climate shifts the ancient snow line, the leopard's silent kingdom keeps shrinking, year after quiet and unnoticed year. Protecting such a rare creature, scientists increasingly agree, simply cannot succeed without the local people who share these harsh and beautiful mountains. (5)______ When villagers are fairly compensated for the occasional lost sheep, they become proud guardians of the leopard rather than its frightened and resentful enemies. In this fragile partnership may lie the last and best hope for the ghost of the mountains.
bank: Few animals on earth are as beautifully and completely suited to a life among snow and bare stone.
Studying it directly was, for a very long time, almost impossibly difficult and sometimes genuinely dangerous.
Capturing a wild leopard and keeping it in a comfortable city zoo is plainly the kindest solution available.
Tools that once seemed like science fiction have transformed how closely we can follow these elusive hunters.
Its high, frozen habitat is being squeezed from several directions all at the very same time.
Snow is generally whiter and softer than the grey rocks that surround it on the highest peaks.
Their cooperation, born of respect rather than fear, may decide whether the species survives at all.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选A（地球上少有动物如此完美适应雪与岩的生活，承接生活在高山的雪豹）；空2选B（长久以来直接研究它几乎不可能且危险，解释为何最不被了解）；空3选D（曾如科幻的工具改变了我们追踪它的能力，承接现代技术揭开面纱）；空4选E（它的高寒栖息地正被多方挤压，承接揭示动物承受压力）；空5选G（这种基于尊重而非恐惧的合作或将决定物种存亡，承接离不开当地人）。干扰句C、F（捉进动物园最仁慈、雪比岩石更白）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Walk into almost any busy cafe today and you will quickly see a long row of people staring silently into their glowing screens, physically together at the very same table yet somehow entirely alone. (1)______ Digital messages have certainly made it wonderfully easy to stay in constant touch across great distances, and yet many of us quietly sense that something warm and genuinely important is slowly slipping away from us. The crucial difference lies in the quality, and not merely the sheer quantity, of our many daily connections. (2)______ A long, unhurried, face-to-face conversation, complete with real laughter and all the small awkward pauses, nourishes the human heart in a way that a hundred quick thumbs-up icons never quite can. Part of the deeper problem is that bright screens reward us with tiny, instant little bursts of cheap attention. (3)______ Each little notification offers a brief flicker of feeling noticed by someone, but these scattered sparks rarely add up to the deep, steady glow of being genuinely and fully known by another person. Building real closeness, by sharp contrast, asks for something that our hurried modern life rarely encourages at all: unhurried, undivided, patient time. (4)______ It means putting the phone face-down on the table, looking someone fully in the eye, and listening carefully without already planning your own clever reply. Such precious moments cannot be rushed, neatly scheduled in tidy five-minute slots, or replaced by any clever new piece of software. (5)______ The friendships and family bonds that truly sustain us are built slowly, through countless small daily acts of patient, undistracted attention. In the end, no glowing device, however brilliant, can ever substitute for the simple, irreplaceable act of being fully present with another human being.
bank: Buying everyone in the room a faster phone would surely make all of them feel far less lonely.
We are, by almost every measurable standard, more connected now than at any moment in human history.
Not all forms of contact, it turns out, feed our deep need for closeness in equal measure.
Online friendships are scientifically proven to be far deeper than any relationship formed in person.
That fragmented kind of attention, pleasant as it feels, is a thin and ultimately unsatisfying substitute.
Genuine presence means giving another person your whole attention rather than a small distracted fraction of it.
They cannot be downloaded, automated, or hurried, only earned through the patient gift of real time.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（按几乎所有标准我们比历史任何时候都更连接，承接随时联系却觉得缺失）；空2选C（并非所有联系都同等满足亲近的需求，承接质量而非数量）；空3选E（那种碎片化的注意是单薄的替代，承接屏幕给予零散的关注）；空4选F（真正的在场意味着给对方全部注意，解释专注不分心的时间）；空5选G（它们无法下载、自动化或催促，只能用真实时间换取，呼应无法被软件替代）。干扰句A、D（给大家买更快手机、网友比线下更深）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Every single autumn, vast flocks of birds rise gracefully into the open sky and set off on incredible journeys of thousands of miles, crossing wide oceans and burning deserts to reach distant lands they have somehow never forgotten. (1)______ How a small creature weighing no more than a handful of soft feathers can navigate such enormous distances with such breathtaking precision has deeply puzzled curious scientists for a very long time indeed. Several clever clues have gradually emerged from many decades of patient observation and careful experiment. (2)______ Many birds appear to read the changing position of the setting sun, memorize the patterns of distant stars, and even somehow sense the invisible magnetic field of the earth itself, weaving all these faint signals together into one reliable inner compass. The precise timing of these journeys is no less remarkable than the long routes they so faithfully follow. (3)______ Shortening autumn days seem to flip a hidden switch deep inside each bird, suddenly filling it with restless energy and an almost irresistible urge to leave long before the first real cold ever arrives. Yet these ancient invisible highways in the sky now face an array of brand-new dangers. (4)______ Bright city lights badly confuse the star-reading travelers, tall glass towers loom invisibly in their path, and the warming climate quietly shifts the very seasons they have trusted for countless generations. Protecting migration, therefore, means thinking far beyond any single national border or boundary. (5)______ A marsh drained carelessly in one country can quietly starve birds that breed safely thousands of miles away, which is exactly why true conservation demands genuine cooperation between many distant nations. Only together can people keep these astonishing journeys flying safely on into the future.
bank: These tiny travelers possess a navigation system far more sophisticated than its modest size would ever suggest.
Feeding migrating birds large amounts of bread is clearly the most effective way to support their long flights.
It is not distance alone but the uncanny accuracy of their departure that astonishes researchers most of all.
Birds are usually lighter than the mammals that share the same wide skies and open landscapes.
Human activity has scattered fresh obstacles all along routes that once stretched safe and clear.
Because the same flock may visit a dozen countries, no single government can possibly protect it alone.
Researchers believe these birds rely not on one sense but on a rich combination of several together.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选A（这些小旅客拥有远超体型的导航系统，承接如何精准导航的疑问）；空2选G（研究者认为它们靠多种感官结合，总起下文太阳、星星、磁场的细节）；空3选C（令研究者最惊讶的是出发时机的精准而非距离，承接迁徙时机同样惊人）；空4选E（人类活动在曾经安全的路线上撒下新障碍，总起下文城市灯光、玻璃塔的危险）；空5选F（同一群鸟可能造访十几个国家，没有单一政府能独自保护，承接需要跨国合作）。干扰句B、D（喂面包最有效、鸟比哺乳动物轻）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: For most of human history, eating together was a slow, shared, and unhurried affair, gathered warmly around a single table with conversation flowing just as freely as the food itself. (1)______ Today, by sharp contrast, an astonishing number of our daily meals are swallowed alone, hurriedly, in front of a flickering screen, with the eater barely noticing what is even passing across the fork. Nutrition experts, interestingly, have begun to worry rather less about exactly what we eat and rather more about how we actually eat it. (2)______ When our attention is scattered carelessly across a phone or a television, the brain quietly fails to register that the stomach is already comfortably full, and so the hand keeps reaching mechanically for more. The simple practice of eating mindfully offers a gentle and surprisingly effective remedy for this. (3)______ By slowing right down, chewing properly, and actually tasting each separate mouthful, a person gives the body the few quiet minutes it badly needs to send its honest signals of fullness. The benefits of this small habit, it turns out, reach well beyond the waistline alone. (4)______ A meal eaten slowly and with real, focused attention is simply far more satisfying, leaving people calmer, more content, and far less likely to go hunting for a snack barely an hour later. None of this requires any expensive food or any complicated and fashionable new diet. (5)______ It asks only that we switch off the screen, sit down properly at the table, and remember the old, almost forgotten pleasure of paying full attention to a single ordinary meal. In such a hurried world, that one quiet act may be among the kindest things we can ever do for ourselves.
bank: Eating as fast as humanly possible is widely recommended as the best way to enjoy any meal.
Something quietly valuable has been lost in this gradual drift toward fast and distracted eating.
Distracted eating, the research increasingly suggests, leads us to consume far more than we actually need.
Drinking several large cups of coffee with every meal is the surest path to lasting good health.
The way we pay attention, or fail to, turns out to shape our health more than we ever guessed.
Such awareness, modest as it sounds, can transform an ordinary meal into something genuinely nourishing.
Indeed, the deepest reward of slow eating may be emotional rather than purely physical at all.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（在这种快速分心进食的漂移中丢失了珍贵的东西，对比过去的共餐）；空2选C（分心进食让我们吃得远超所需，承接专家更关注怎么吃）；空3选F（这种觉察能把普通一餐变成真正滋养，承接正念进食的做法）；空4选G（慢食最深的回报或是情感而非纯生理，承接好处超出身材）；空5选E（我们如何专注最终比想象更影响健康，呼应不需贵食材也能改变，引出关掉屏幕）。干扰句A、D（尽量快吃最享受、每餐喝咖啡最健康）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: When a major new highway was finally proposed to cut straight through the quiet edge of an old residential neighborhood, the residents there reacted not with helpless anger but with an unusual and quiet determination. (1)______ Instead of simply complaining loudly to one another over their garden fences, they calmly decided to study the official plans line by careful line and to understand exactly what was truly at stake for them. What they gradually discovered surprised even the most patient and reasonable among them. (2)______ The planners, it turned out, had quietly assumed all along that this particular ordinary community would never organize itself well enough to ask a single difficult or inconvenient question. The residents began, modestly, by holding open public meetings in the small local library every single week. (3)______ Engineers, retired schoolteachers, and curious teenagers all sat together at the same long wooden table, slowly piecing together a clear and convincing alternative route that nobody in power had ever once bothered to consider. Presenting their careful counter-proposal demanded real personal courage and a great deal of plain, unglamorous hard work. (4)______ Standing before a crowded room of skeptical officials and defending many months of voluntary research is, after all, far more frightening than signing any quick petition could ever possibly be. In the end, their patient, evidence-based campaign achieved something that is genuinely rare. (5)______ The final route was quietly shifted to spare both the oldest trees and the busiest children's playground, proving that ordinary citizens, when properly organized, can still meaningfully shape the very places they call home. Real civic power, it seems, belongs to those willing to do the slow and thoroughly unglamorous homework.
bank: Quietly accepting whatever the authorities decided was, of course, by far the easiest path available to them.
From the very start, they sensed that informed questions would carry more weight than mere shouting ever could.
Their early calm had been badly mistaken by officials for simple indifference or helpless ignorance.
These weekly gatherings gradually grew into a genuine workshop of shared local knowledge and skill.
Highways are generally wider and noisier than the quiet residential streets they so often replace.
Yet the volunteers pushed through their nerves, convinced that their argument deserved a fair hearing.
Officials eventually agreed to redesign the route, persuaded not by anger but by careful, undeniable evidence.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（他们从一开始就感到有理有据的提问比喊叫更有分量，承接以安静的决心而非愤怒回应）；空2选C（官员把他们早期的冷静误当作冷漠无知，承接连最有耐心者都吃惊的发现）；空3选D（每周的聚会渐成共享本地知识的工作坊，承接在图书馆开公开会议）；空4选F（志愿者克服紧张，相信论点值得被听见，承接呈交反提案需要勇气）；空5选G（官员最终被严谨证据而非愤怒说服而重新设计路线，承接取得罕见成果）。干扰句A、E（默默接受最省事、高速比住宅街更宽更吵）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Across the busy open floors of modern offices everywhere, an old and stubborn debate has quietly flared up once again: is it really better to focus deeply on a single task or to juggle many of them at once? (1)______ The familiar image of the heroic multitasker, answering urgent emails while joining a conference call and skimming a long report, has long been admired as a proud symbol of boundless energy and modern efficiency. Careful laboratory studies, however, tell a rather different and far less flattering story about all this. (2)______ Each time the busy mind jumps from one demanding task to another, it pays a small hidden toll in lost focus, and these tiny scattered tolls quietly add up across a single long working day. The human brain, it turns out, is not nearly as good at true parallel work as we proudly like to believe it is. (3)______ What actually happens instead is a rapid, exhausting switching back and forth, with a thin lingering layer of confusion clinging stubbornly to every single handover. Single-tasking, by sharp contrast, often feels noticeably slower and almost old-fashioned to us. (4)______ Yet by giving one single task your whole, undivided mind, you finish it faster, make far fewer careless errors, and end the long day feeling noticeably less frazzled and drained. Breaking the deep-rooted multitasking habit is genuinely difficult in a modern world that is built for constant interruption. (5)______ Turning off all the needless notifications, closing the extra browser tabs, and carefully protecting short blocks of quiet time can slowly retrain even the most scattered and restless modern mind. The deepest and finest work, in the end, almost always belongs to those who can give it their full and patient attention.
bank: Doing six demanding things at once is unquestionably the proven secret of every truly successful worker.
For years, this question seemed to have an obvious and almost universally accepted answer in its favour.
Researchers consistently find that switching between tasks costs us far more time than it ever appears to save.
What we casually call multitasking is mostly just a clumsy illusion the busy brain creates for itself.
Buying a second large computer monitor will instantly double anyone's true productivity at work.
It lacks the impressive, busy appearance that frantic juggling so effortlessly puts on display.
The good news is that focus, much like a muscle, can be patiently strengthened with steady practice.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（多年来这问题似乎有个几乎公认的偏向答案，承接对多任务英雄的崇拜）；空2选C（研究一致发现任务切换耗费的时间远超表面所省，引出隐性代价）；空3选D（我们随口说的多任务多半是大脑制造的笨拙错觉，承接大脑并不擅长并行）；空4选F（它缺少手忙脚乱那种忙碌的表象，承接单任务显得慢而过时）；空5选G（好消息是专注像肌肉可被稳步练强，承接打破多任务习惯虽难，引出关通知等做法）。干扰句A、E（同时做六件是成功秘诀、买第二块显示器翻倍效率）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: On a quiet and windswept stretch of northern coastline, a small group of fishing families recently faced a hard and frightening truth: the once-rich waters they had trusted for many generations were slowly falling strangely silent. (1)______ Year after long year, the heavy nets came up lighter and lighter, the fish grew steadily smaller, and the old, easy confidence of the little harbor slowly curdled into a gnawing worry. Rather than simply blame distant factories or shrug helplessly at unkind fate, the close community deliberately chose a far braver path. (2)______ They agreed, after many long and sometimes heated evening meetings, to stop fishing entirely in one carefully chosen bay and to let those troubled waters rest completely for several full years. The difficult decision cost them dearly in the short term. (3)______ Families who had always lived hand to mouth from the daily catch now had to tighten their belts painfully and trust quietly in a recovery that no one on earth could absolutely guarantee. Slowly and almost invisibly, however, something quietly hopeful began to stir beneath the cold grey waves. (4)______ Young fish, finally left wholly undisturbed, multiplied happily in the protected bay and then spilled outward into the surrounding open waters, gradually refilling the empty nets of the patient families nearby. The encouraging story has since spread to many other struggling villages all along the same long coast. (5)______ What began as one desperate community's frightening gamble has grown into a living example that ordinary people, by acting together with real foresight, can genuinely heal the very waters that sustain them. Sometimes the wisest harvest of all, it turns out, is the one you deliberately choose not to take.
bank: Catching every last remaining fish as quickly as possible was obviously the only rational response left to them.
The signs of decline had been creeping in quietly for far longer than anyone had wanted to admit.
Together they made a sacrifice that demanded real patience and a great deal of shared, stubborn trust.
Letting the sea rest, they discovered, meant accepting genuine hardship before any reward could possibly arrive.
Seawater is generally saltier than the fresh water that flows down from the nearby mountain streams.
Nature, given even half a chance, proved astonishingly generous in repaying their difficult collective patience.
Neighboring fishers, watching the bay slowly come back to life, began carefully copying the very same approach.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（衰退的迹象悄悄潜伏已久无人愿承认，承接水域奇怪地沉寂）；空2选C（他们共同做出需要耐心与顽固信任的牺牲，承接选择更勇敢的路，引出封湾休渔）；空3选D（让海休养意味着先承受真正的艰难才有回报，承接短期代价高昂）；空4选F（大自然只要给半个机会就慷慨回报，承接水下开始有希望的萌动）；空5选G（邻近渔民看着海湾复苏也开始效仿，承接故事传到其他村庄）。干扰句A、E（尽快捕光最后的鱼最理性、海水比山泉咸）放入任一空都不连贯。
